meeting-transcript-summarizer.whyItMatters: Turns long recordings into concise, actionable briefs so teams can execute quickly after client calls.
problem: Project teams were spending too much time manually recapping calls.
Action items were inconsistently captured across different meeting formats.
Stakeholders wanted fast summaries tailored to technical and executive audiences.
approach: Built an ingest pipeline that normalizes transcript chunks and speaker turns.
Implemented prompt templates for recap, decisions, and next-step extraction.
Added post-processing to format outputs into client-ready summary briefs.
result: Recap turnaround moved from manual hours to a repeatable minutes-level workflow.
Follow-up tasks became easier to assign with clearer owner and context formatting.
Summary consistency improved across recurring client engagement meetings.
highlights: Structured output for easy CRM handoff

architecture.summary: Pipeline-first architecture for transcript normalization, prompting, and delivery formatting.
bullets: Ingestion stage standardizes transcript chunks and speaker turns.
Prompt stage emits structured recap + action JSON outputs.
Renderer stage transforms results into client-ready brief formats.

keyFiles.note: Normalizes transcript payloads into analysis-ready segments.
Prompt variants for exec summary, technical recap, and follow-up.
Formats final summaries into a consistent, shareable structure.

meeting-transcript-summarizer.shipped: Shipped owner-aware action-item detection with confidence tagging.
meeting-transcript-summarizer.learned: Codified a repeatable summarization workflow that reduces manual recap work and keeps client communication consistent.
meeting-transcript-summarizer.accessNote: Code is private. I can share a walkthrough and redacted samples on request.

ai-faq-chatbot-prototype.whyItMatters: Demonstrates how AI support flows can improve response quality while reducing repetitive human support load.
problem: Support teams were answering repetitive questions with inconsistent phrasing.
Users needed faster first-response answers without digging through docs.
Initial chatbot drafts were too generic and lacked source-grounded confidence.
approach: Curated domain FAQs into a retrieval-friendly, structured knowledge set.
Designed response templates to keep tone concise, helpful, and support-safe.
Added guardrails for uncertainty handling and escalation when confidence is low.
result: Prototype responses became clearer and more action-oriented for common support intents.
Knowledge-grounded replies reduced hallucination risk on known question paths.
Conversation flow design made human handoff expectations more explicit.

receipts.label: Answer consistency
receipts.value: Prompt templates standardized support response style

architecture.summary: Retrieval-grounded chatbot with confidence routing and escalation behavior.
bullets: Retriever pulls scoped FAQ entries by intent and keywords.
Generator produces support-safe response templates with citations.
Router handles confidence thresholds and escalation transitions.

keyFiles.note: Structured knowledge base used for retrieval and grounding.
Core system and response-style constraints for the assistant.
Routes between answer generation, clarification, and escalation states.

ai-faq-chatbot-prototype.shipped: Added uncertainty-aware routing to prefer safe escalation over low-confidence answers.
ai-faq-chatbot-prototype.learned: Refined prompt and response patterns to produce clearer answers and stronger customer self-service consistency.
ai-faq-chatbot-prototype.accessNote: Prototype code is private. Happy to discuss design tradeoffs and evaluation approach.

client-ecommerce-implementation.whyItMatters: Shows end-to-end delivery across conversion UX, checkout reliability, and production operations for revenue-critical systems.
problem: Storefront friction in checkout was creating unnecessary abandonment risk.
Mobile performance issues affected perceived trust and purchasing momentum.
Operations needed safer release and rollback patterns around campaigns.
approach: Redesigned core purchase paths with clearer hierarchy and fewer checkout steps.
Applied performance tuning across caching, assets, and script loading order.
Implemented launch checklists and runbooks for promotions and incident handling.
result: Checkout flow became more resilient and easier for non-technical teams to operate.
Page responsiveness improved on mobile-heavy paths during campaign traffic.
Deployment and support handoffs became more predictable with documented runbooks.

receipts.label: Checkout resilience
receipts.value: Retry-safe payment validation and fallback messaging

receipts.label: Operational readiness
receipts.value: Launch + rollback runbook coverage for campaigns

architecture.summary: Storefront + payment stack with performance tuning and runbook-backed operations.
bullets: Theme templates optimize conversion on high-intent purchase paths.
Middleware plugins enforce caching and script-load discipline.
Operations layer includes rollout checks and incident response guides.

keyFiles.note: Conversion-focused checkout structure and validation flow.
Caching, script strategy, and request optimization helpers.
Launch, rollback, and on-call guidance for campaign operations.

client-ecommerce-implementation.shipped: Released hardened checkout validation and payment retry handling.
client-ecommerce-implementation.learned: Strengthened delivery discipline across launch readiness, performance, and post-launch support for business-critical storefronts.
client-ecommerce-implementation.accessNote: Client work under NDA. I can share scope, approach, and outcomes at a high level.

lakefront-digital-portfolio.whyItMatters: Captures how engineering and communication design can work together to explain technical work to business audiences.
problem: The prior site did not clearly communicate delivery capabilities and outcomes.
Project proof points were hard to scan across services, blog, and portfolio pages.
Needed a maintainable content model for frequent iteration.
approach: Built a modular React + TypeScript front end with reusable content sections.
Implemented structured project data to support case-study storytelling.
Added design-system primitives to keep visual and interaction patterns consistent.
result: Portfolio pages now emphasize outcomes and decision quality over raw repo listings.
Content updates became faster with structured data-driven sections.
Site architecture now supports ongoing experiments and feature evolution.

receipts.label: Design-system quality
receipts.value: Automated lint gates for token drift + legacy imports

receipts.label: Delivery signal clarity
receipts.value: Portfolio reframed around outcomes and shipped artifacts

architecture.summary: Data-driven React portfolio composed from reusable content + design primitives.
bullets: Structured repo metadata powers grid, timeline, and case-study layouts.
UI primitives centralize motion, cards, badges, and interaction patterns.
Route-level pages keep feature slices cohesive and maintainable.

keyFiles.note: Work showcase layout with filtering, grouping, and timeline sections.
Curated case-study metadata powering rich project storytelling.
Reusable card primitive used across portfolio surfaces.

lakefront-digital-portfolio.shipped: Shipped case-study metadata model for richer portfolio storytelling.
lakefront-digital-portfolio.learned: Applied practical content architecture and UX decisions to clearly communicate technical consulting services.

wordpress-support-runbooks.whyItMatters: Operational documentation can be a force multiplier; this project turns tribal knowledge into reusable support systems.
problem: Recurring support questions consumed disproportionate delivery time.
Incident triage quality varied based on who was on-call.
Client handoffs lacked a consistent troubleshooting format.
approach: Designed reusable runbook templates for common incident categories.
Standardized intake, diagnosis, and escalation pathways.
Added role-based checklists for support and implementation handoffs.
result: Support responses became more consistent and easier to delegate.
Onboarding new contributors required less tribal context transfer.
Documentation quality improved cross-project operational continuity.

receipts.label: Support consistency
receipts.value: Runbook-driven triage format across recurring incidents

receipts.label: Onboarding speed
receipts.value: New contributors use templates instead of tribal context

architecture.summary: Documentation system centered on intake, escalation, and post-incident loops.
bullets: Intake templates normalize issue context before diagnosis begins.
Escalation matrix codifies ownership by severity level.
Retro templates capture prevention actions and follow-through items.

keyFiles.note: Standard issue intake structure for faster triage.
Defines escalation ownership and severity thresholds.
Template for documenting lessons and prevention actions.

wordpress-support-runbooks.shipped: Added severity-based escalation matrix and post-incident retro template.
wordpress-support-runbooks.learned: Standardized setup and troubleshooting guidance so support can move faster with fewer recurring clarifications.
wordpress-support-runbooks.accessNote: Runbooks are internal templates. Available as redacted examples on request.
